content.full_text:
March 30, 2005

Kevin A. Buford
Holland & Knight LLP
2099 Pennsylvania Ave., N.W., Suite 100
Washington D.C. 20006

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-05-048

Dear Mr. Buford:

This letter confirms the receipt of your March 14, 2005 request for advice. Under the provisions of California Code of Regulations section 18329(b)(8)(D), the FPPC will decline to provide written advice, where an inquiry is hypothetical in nature. Moreover, the determination of whether an outside contractor is a "consultant" under a state agency's conflict of interest code, is a determination made by the contracting agency. (Regulation 18329.5(a)(2).) We recommend that you consult with the Department of Health Services concerning the content of your questionnaire.

I have enclosed a copy of regulation 18329 for your review. Also enclosed is regulation 18701, Murabito Advice Letter, No. A-04-245, Wasko Advice Letter, No. A-04-270, Benardy Advice Letter, No. I-04-041, all of which pertain to the definition of "consultant" under the Political Reform Act.

You may contact our toll-free help line with general questions at 1-866-275-3772.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: John W. Wallace
Assistant General Counsel
